en: A: Is Kiua at home, please?
She’s at home, please come in.
A: Is Kiua at home, please?

ab: ’akia nguain tanasa, ’una kara tia kari musu?
ch: B:她不在，請問你有什麼事?
en: B: She’s not here, what can I do for you?
B: Who is it?

ab: tamu, sua iku ia Paicʉ. putu’ʉnʉvoo pa!
en: A: Grandpa, I’m Paicʉ, please open the door.
B: Who is it?

ab: ’Avia, sua iku ia ’Angai. tikirimi ku kasua sumasima’ʉ.
en: A: ’Avia, I’m ’Angai, I’ve come to play with you.
A: Long time no see.

ab: ʉʉ’ʉ ia! pacʉpʉngʉ kasua, cii! ivataa parʉ’ʉna.
ch: B:是啊!我很想念你。快來聊天吧!
en: B:Yeah! I miss you so much. Come on, let’s chat!
B: Please sit down and have some tea.
A: Thank you.
B: Please have some tea.
A:Your tea is delicious.
B: Let’s eat fruit!

ab: ʉʉ’ʉ! makai sa’o’o cʉ’ʉrʉʉn macu’u isi.
ch: B:好喔!這些水果看起來很棒。
en: A: Yes! These fruits look great.
B: How is the fruit?
A:Yes, it’s really delicious.

ab: nipo’ucipi cu cina tia kʉnʉa rovana, ivataa cu kumakʉnʉ!
ch: A:媽媽煮好晚餐了，快來吃吧!
en: B: Mom has cooked dinner, come and eat!
A: Okay, I’ll go wash my hands.

ch: A:盡量吃喝，你又不是外人。
en: B: Try to eat and drink as much as possible, you’re not an outsider.
A: I ate a lot, I’m full.
B: Eat more, don’t be polite.
A:Well, it’s delicious.

ab: tia cu ku aratʉkʉnʉ, maka’ʉna taniara pai cumacʉ’ʉra kasua.
ch: A:我要離開了，下次再來看你。
en: A: I’m leaving, I’ll see you next time.
B: Okay, goodbye.

ab: rovana cu! tia cu ku pui’i tanasa.
en: A: It’s late, I’m going home.
B:Come again next time!
B: Walk slowly and carefully.

ab: ʉʉ’ʉ! ivataa tanasa maku rovʉvʉ noo ’una tapaicaa.
en: A: Okay, come to my house sometime.
